report_name: CL-STA-1062: Chinese-Speaking Threat Actor Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Critical Infrastructure with New TinyRCT Backdoor

threat_actors.name: CL-STA-1062
threat_actors.description: A Chinese-speaking threat actor conducting a sustained espionage campaign against government and critical energy infrastructure entities in Southeast Asia since at least March 2022. The group uses a mix of open-source tools and a custom backdoor named TinyRCT.
threat_actors.goals: Long-term intelligence gathering and data exfiltration from strategic targets in the Asia-Pacific region.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The primary initial access vector used by CL-STA-1062 is the exploitation of vulnerabilities in public-facing web applications to gain a foothold in the target network.

attack_patterns.name: Server Software Component: Web Shell
attack_patterns.description: CL-STA-1062 deploys ASPX web shells on compromised servers after successful exploitation. These web shells are used for command execution, reconnaissance, and deployment of further tools.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor used web shells to interact with the underlying operating system and execute arbitrary commands via cmd.exe.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The group used the JuicyPotato tool to escalate privileges on compromised Windows systems.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory
attack_patterns.description: CL-STA-1062 used Mimikatz to dump credentials from the LSASS process memory to facilitate lateral movement.

attack_patterns.name: Proxy
attack_patterns.description: The attackers relied on various tunneling tools, including SoftEther VPN, VNT, and yuze, to maintain command and control and exfiltrate data, often disguising them with legitimate-sounding names.

attack_patterns.name: Data Staged: Local Data Staging
attack_patterns.description: Before exfiltration, the threat actor compressed stolen data into password-protected RAR archives on the compromised system.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Stolen data, including database contents and source code, was exfiltrated through the command and control tunnels established by the attackers.

malware.name: TinyRCT
malware.description: A new, lightweight, custom .NET backdoor used by CL-STA-1062 for stealth and persistence. It provides capabilities for arbitrary command execution, file exfiltration, screenshot capture, and remote control.

malware.name: ASPX web shells
malware.description: Used by CL-STA-1062 as a primary mechanism after exploiting web applications to execute commands, perform reconnaissance, and deploy additional tools.

tools.name: SoftEther VPN
tools.description: A tunneling tool used by CL-STA-1062 for command and control and data exfiltration.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Used by CL-STA-1062 to dump credentials from LSASS memory.

tools.name: JuicyPotato
tools.description: A privilege escalation tool used by CL-STA-1062 on compromised Windows systems.

tools.name: VNT
tools.description: A tunneling tool used by CL-STA-1062 for command and control and data exfiltration.

tools.name: yuze
tools.description: A tunneling tool used by CL-STA-1062 for command and control and data exfiltration.

tools.name: curl
tools.description: Used by the attackers to send enumeration results directly to an actor-controlled IP address.

tools.name: rar
tools.description: Used by the attackers to compress stolen data into password-protected archives before exfiltration.

identities.name: Southeast Asian government entities
identities.description: A primary target class for the CL-STA-1062 espionage campaign.

identities.name: Southeast Asian critical energy infrastructure entities
identities.description: A primary target class for the CL-STA-1062 espionage campaign, including two state-owned providers.

identities.name: East Asian strategic sector entities
identities.description: Targets of earlier operations by CL-STA-1062, indicating a broad regional focus.

identities.name: Taiwanese web hosting infrastructure
identities.description: Targets of earlier operations by CL-STA-1062.

infrastructure.name: TinyRCT C2 Server
infrastructure.description: A server at 139.180.134.221 was found hosting the TinyRCT backdoor, named PerfWatson2.exe.

indicators.name: TinyRCT C2 Server Connection
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: TinyRCT C2 Server Connection
id: 9f8e7d6c-5b4a-4321-9876-fedcba987654
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network connections to a known C2 server used by the CL-STA-1062 threat actor to host the TinyRCT backdoor.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cl-sta-1062-tinyrct-backdoor/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1071
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationIp: '139.180.134.221'
    Initiated: true
  condition: selection

indicators.name: TinyRCT Backdoor Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: TinyRCT Backdoor Execution (PerfWatson2.exe)
id: 8e7d6c5b-4a32-4109-8765-edcba9876543
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PerfWatson2.exe, which is the filename used for the TinyRCT backdoor by CL-STA-1062.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cl-sta-1062-tinyrct-backdoor/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\PerfWatson2.exe'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: CL-STA-1062 Tunneling Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: CL-STA-1062 Tunneling Tool Execution
id: 7d6c5b4a-3210-4987-6543-dcba98765432
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of known tunneling tools (yuze.exe, vnt.exe) used by the CL-STA-1062 threat actor.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cl-sta-1062-tinyrct-backdoor/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1090
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\yuze.exe'
      - '\vnt.exe'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: Suspicious RAR Archiving on Server
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious RAR Archiving on Server
id: 6c5b4a32-1098-4765-4321-cba987654321
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of rar.exe to create password-protected archives, a technique used by CL-STA-1062 for data staging before exfiltration.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cl-sta-1062-tinyrct-backdoor/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rar.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' a '
      - ' -p'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects command-line usage of rar.exe to create password-protected archives, a TTP of CL-STA-1062 for staging data.

indicators.name: Web Shell Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: ASPX Web Shell Creation
id: 5b4a3210-9876-4543-2109-ba9876543210
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of new ASPX files in web-accessible directories, which could indicate web shell deployment by actors like CL-STA-1062.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cl-sta-1062-tinyrct-backdoor/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.aspx'
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - ':\inetpub\wwwroot\'
      - ':\inetpub\logs\'
  filter_legit_deploys:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\devenv.exe'
      - '\msbuild.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_deploys
indicators.description: Detects the creation of .aspx files in common web directories, a primary technique of CL-STA-1062 for persistence.

relationships.target_name: Southeast Asian critical energy infrastructure entities
Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell
